title: Ebola Outbreak Grows in Congo as Experts Call for Urgent Action
subtitle: Health workers face difficult conditions in the epicenter while international agencies coordinate a response to stop the virus from spreading further.

summary.en: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo continues to grow, with over 500 confirmed cases reported. Health authorities are struggling to manage the disease due to limited resources and the difficult environment in the Ituri province. Medical workers are working under extreme pressure with little pay or rest. International agencies have launched a large financial plan to support the response over the next six months. Meanwhile, neighboring Uganda is working to contain a small number of cases that crossed the border.
summary.zh: 剛果民主共和國的伊波拉疫情持續擴大，確診病例已超過五百例。由於資源有限且伊圖里省環境惡劣，衛生當局在控制疫情上備感艱辛。醫護人員在薪資微薄且缺乏休息的情況下承受極大壓力。國際機構已啟動一項大型財務計畫，以支援未來六個月的防疫工作。與此同時，鄰國烏干達正努力控制少量跨境傳入的病例。

paragraphs.en: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) is spreading at a fast pace, causing deep concern among health officials. According to NPR Health, the country has confirmed 515 cases of the virus, with 91 deaths recorded so far. The situation is particularly difficult in the Ituri province, where more than 94% of these cases are located. This region is known for its lack of paved roads and the presence of violent armed groups, which makes it very hard for aid workers to reach those who need help.
paragraphs.zh: 剛果民主共和國 (Democratic Republic of Congo) 的伊波拉病毒疫情正迅速蔓延，引起衛生官員的高度擔憂。根據美國公共廣播電台 (NPR) 健康頻道的報導，該國已確認五百一十五起病例，目前記錄到九十一例死亡。伊圖里省 (Ituri) 的情況尤為嚴峻，超過百分之九十四的病例集中於此。該地區以缺乏鋪設道路及暴力武裝團體盤據而聞名，這使得救援人員難以接觸到需要幫助的民眾。

paragraphs.en: MedPage Today reports that the outbreak began in the busy gold mining area of Mongbwalu. The working conditions there are a major concern for health experts. Laborers often work in deep, muddy pits and live in crowded, low-income camps. Because the virus spreads through close contact with the bodily fluids of sick people—such as blood, sweat, or vomit—these crowded living conditions make it much easier for the disease to move from person to person. Many people in the area are also skeptical about the disease, which makes the work of doctors and nurses even more challenging.
paragraphs.zh: 根據醫療新聞網站 MedPage Today 的報導，疫情始於繁忙的蒙布瓦盧 (Mongbwalu) 金礦區。當地的勞動條件是衛生專家的一大隱憂。工人們常在深且泥濘的坑洞中工作，並居住在擁擠的低收入營地。由於病毒是透過接觸病患的體液（如血液、汗水或嘔吐物）傳播，這些擁擠的居住環境使得疾病更容易在人與人之間擴散。此外，當地許多民眾對該疾病抱持懷疑態度，這讓醫師與護理師的工作更加艱鉅。

paragraphs.en: Medical staff are currently working under extreme pressure. Dr. Richard Lokudu, a medical director at a local hospital, told STAT News that he and his colleagues have been treating a constant flow of patients, often working all day and night. Despite the high risks they face, many of these workers have not received their regular pay. Heather Kerr, a director for the International Rescue Committee in the DRC, noted that the health system has been weak for many years due to a lack of investment. This has left hospitals with very few supplies, such as masks, gloves, and basic medicine, at the start of the crisis.
paragraphs.zh: 醫護人員目前承受著極大的壓力。當地一家醫院的醫療主任洛庫杜醫師 (Dr. Richard Lokudu) 向 STAT News 表示，他和同事們必須不斷處理湧入的病患，經常日以繼夜地工作。儘管面臨高度風險，許多工作人員卻未能領到應有的薪資。國際救援委員會 (International Rescue Committee) 在剛果民主共和國的負責人克爾 (Heather Kerr) 指出，由於長期缺乏投資，當地的醫療體系早已積弱不振。這導致危機爆發之初，醫院極度缺乏口罩、手套與基本藥物等物資。

paragraphs.en: In response to the growing threat, international organizations are stepping up their efforts. Deutsche Welle (DW) Top reports that the World Health Organization and the Africa CDC have launched a plan worth over 500 million dollars. This money is intended to help control the outbreak over the next six months. The virus involved in this outbreak is the rare Bundibugyo type, for which there is currently no approved vaccine. This makes the job of medical teams much harder, as they must focus on treating symptoms rather than preventing the disease with a shot.
paragraphs.zh: 為了應對日益嚴峻的威脅，國際組織正加強行動。德國之聲 (DW) 報導指出，世界衛生組織 (WHO) 與非洲疾病管制與預防中心 (Africa CDC) 已啟動一項價值超過五億美元的計畫。這筆資金旨在協助控制未來六個月的疫情。此次爆發的病毒屬於罕見的本迪布焦 (Bundibugyo) 型，目前尚無核准的疫苗。這使得醫療團隊的工作更加困難，因為他們必須專注於治療症狀，而非透過疫苗接種來預防疾病。

paragraphs.en: While the situation in the DRC is serious, neighboring Uganda is also dealing with the virus. According to reports from Deutsche Welle (DW) Top, Uganda has recorded 19 cases, including two deaths. The head of the World Health Organization recently visited Uganda to praise the country for its quick response. He noted that border screening and strong testing systems have helped Uganda keep the number of cases low. However, he also warned against closing borders completely, suggesting that such actions may not be the most effective way to stop the virus.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管剛果民主共和國的情況嚴重，鄰國烏干達也正處理該病毒的威脅。根據德國之聲的報導，烏干達已記錄到十九起病例，其中包括兩例死亡。世界衛生組織負責人近期造訪烏干達，讚揚該國的快速反應。他指出，邊境篩檢與完善的檢測系統協助烏干達將病例數維持在低點。然而，他也警告不應完全封鎖邊境，並表示這類手段未必是阻斷病毒最有效的方式。

paragraphs.en: There is some small hope as a few patients have begun to recover. Congolese health authorities confirmed that several people have left the hospital after getting better. However, experts warn that the official numbers might not show the full picture. Because the region is remote and often affected by conflict, it is very difficult to test everyone who might be sick. The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention recently created a model to predict how far the virus could spread. They warned that if only a small percentage of patients are isolated—kept away from others to prevent spread—the number of cases could rise to over 20,000 in just three months.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著少數病患開始康復，情況出現了一絲希望。剛果衛生當局證實，已有數人在病情好轉後出院。不過，專家警告官方數據可能未能呈現全貌。由於該地區偏遠且常受衝突影響，要檢測所有潛在病患極為困難。美國疾病管制與預防中心 (CDC) 近期建立了一個模型，用以預測病毒的擴散範圍。他們警告，若僅有少數病患被隔離（即與他人分開以防止傳播），病例數在短短三個月內可能激增至超過兩萬例。

paragraphs.en: As the government of the DRC works to manage the crisis, they have brought back travel restrictions to and from the city of Bunia. While the government insists that the situation is under control, the combination of a weak health system, difficult geography, and the ongoing threat of violence means that the coming months will be a major test for the region. For now, the priority remains providing better support for the tired health workers on the front lines and ensuring that medical supplies reach the most affected areas.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著剛果民主共和國政府致力於處理這場危機，他們已恢復對布尼亞市 (Bunia) 的往返旅遊限制。儘管政府堅稱情況已在掌控之中，但醫療體系脆弱、地理環境險惡以及持續存在的暴力威脅，意味著未來幾個月將是該地區的一大考驗。目前，首要任務仍是為前線疲憊的醫護人員提供更好的支援，並確保醫療物資能送達受災最嚴重的地區。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To increase the amount, speed, or intensity of an activity.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於日常對話，這裡指增加行動的強度。文中：In response to the growing threat, international organizations are stepping up their efforts.

keyTerms.definitionEn: An injection of a medicine or vaccine.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「射擊」或「嘗試」，這裡指醫學上的注射。文中：This makes the job of medical teams much harder, as they must focus on treating symptoms rather than preventing the disease with a shot.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A difficult situation that shows how strong or capable someone or something is.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「考試」或「檢測」，這裡指對能力或耐力的嚴峻考驗。文中：the combination of a weak health system, difficult geography, and the ongoing threat of violence means that the coming months will be a major test for the region.

previousArticles.title: Health Experts Warn of Potential Ebola Crisis in Central Africa
previousArticles.summaryEn: US health officials have released new data suggesting the current Ebola outbreak in Central Africa could grow significantly. Models from the CDC indicate that cases could reach 20,000 or more if the spread is not controlled. The situation is complicated by ongoing armed conflict in the region, which makes it difficult for health workers to reach those in need. While these projections are serious, some experts warn that predicting the exact path of an outbreak is very difficult. International health organizations are currently working to manage the crisis and prevent further loss of life.

previousArticles.title: New Warning Issued as Ebola Outbreak Grows in Central Africa
previousArticles.summaryEn: US health officials have released new computer models showing that the current Ebola outbreak in Central Africa could become as large as the 2014 record. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) warns that cases could reach 20,000 or more if isolation efforts are not improved. The situation is made more difficult by ongoing armed conflict in the region. Experts note that while the data is worrying, predicting the exact path of an outbreak remains very challenging. International health organizations are currently working to contain the virus and prevent further spread.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason the Ituri province is considered a difficult area for aid workers to operate in?

options.A: The local population is actively hostile toward all foreign medical staff.
options.B: The region suffers from poor infrastructure and the presence of armed conflict.
options.C: The gold mining industry has prohibited access to the most affected villages.
options.D: The lack of available vaccines makes the area too dangerous for medical personnel.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段，Ituri 省因為缺乏鋪設好的道路（lack of paved roads）以及暴力武裝團體（violent armed groups）的存在，導致援助人員難以進入。選項 A 過度推論，文中僅提到民眾對疾病抱持懷疑態度，並未說對所有人員敵對；選項 C 文中未提及礦業禁止進入；選項 D 雖然提到缺乏疫苗，但這不是導致該地區難以進入的主要地理或安全原因。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why might the actual number of Ebola cases in the DRC be higher than the officially reported 515?

options.A: The government is intentionally hiding the true scale of the outbreak to avoid panic.
options.B: Medical staff are too busy to record the deaths of patients who have already passed away.
options.C: Geographic isolation and regional instability prevent comprehensive testing and reporting.
options.D: The Bundibugyo virus is so rare that standard testing kits are unable to detect it at all.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第六段，專家警告官方數據可能無法呈現全貌，原因是該地區偏遠且受衝突影響，難以對所有可能患病的人進行檢測。選項 A 文中並未提到政府蓄意隱瞞；選項 B 雖然提到醫護人員壓力大，但並未說他們沒在記錄死亡人數；選項 D 雖然提到該病毒罕見且無疫苗，但並未說檢測工具完全無法偵測。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'test' mean in the phrase 'the coming months will be a major test for the region'?

options.A: A medical examination to detect the presence of a virus.
options.B: A difficult situation that challenges the strength or ability of someone or something.
options.C: A scientific experiment conducted to prove a new theory.
options.D: A formal assessment used to measure the knowledge of health workers.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。在此文中，「test」並非醫學檢測或考試，而是指一場嚴峻的考驗或挑戰，用來評估該地區在面對醫療系統薄弱、地理困難與暴力威脅下的承受能力。選項 A 是常見的干擾項，但在句中不符合語境；選項 C 與 D 雖然是 test 的常見含義，但在該段落描述區域危機的脈絡下，明顯不適用。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the Ebola outbreak in the DRC?

options.A: The Ebola outbreak is successfully being contained due to international funding and support.
options.B: The primary cause of the outbreak is the poor working conditions in gold mines.
options.C: The outbreak is a complex crisis exacerbated by systemic, geographic, and security challenges.
options.D: Uganda's response to the virus serves as a perfect model for the DRC to follow immediately.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文綜合討論了剛果民主共和國（DRC）疫情的嚴峻性，並強調了醫療系統脆弱、地理環境惡劣、武裝衝突以及民眾懷疑態度等因素，這些都使得危機處理變得極其困難。選項 A 與文章事實不符，目前情況仍非常嚴峻；選項 B 僅是疫情的一個起因，而非全文主旨；選項 D 雖然提到烏干達的經驗，但文章並未將其視為 DRC 可直接複製的唯一解決方案。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
